hero.quote_before: Our competitors have never had to fold up a screen at 3am to go to bed.

products.section_desc: Two technologies, one obsession: lightness and reliability. 30 years of field experience in every seam.
products.etanche_desc: Inspired by kitesurfing. Sealed air chamber, single inflation in 3 minutes, no blower required. Ultra-light and transportable by one person.
products.soufflerie_desc: Dupont de Nemours car airbag fabric. Permanent blower for very large formats. Stows in a sail bag without folding. 10-year guarantee.
products.tentes_desc: Same technology as our screens. Quick assembly, no tools, no blower required.
products.mobilier_desc: Sofas, armchairs, counters. Lightweight, portable, elegant.
products.title: Our products
products.subtitle: A complete range for all your events
products.ecrans_desc: From 2m to 24m, the lightest screens in the world. Blower or airtight technology.
products.arches_desc: Inflatable arches for sports and promotional events.
products.mobilier_desc2: Armchairs, sofas and inflatable poufs for your lounge areas.

technology.quote_1_before: "I don't even need a crane to move me.
technology.quote_2: I arrived alone in a 12m³ van and I'm leaving alone for a well-deserved rest until my next session.
technology.story_title: From defence secrets to the silver screen
technology.story_p1_before: It all began in Lyon, the historic capital of textiles. While searching for a fabric that was both light and indestructible, we discovered that the
technology.story_p1_after: used exactly what we were looking for.
technology.story_p2_before: The manufacturers told us it was "secret defence". We didn't give up. After months of investigation, we identified the fabric: a
technology.story_p2_highlight: high tenacity polyamide from Dupont de Nemours
technology.story_p3: For the airtight range, the inspiration came from elsewhere: on a beach in Hong Kong, watching kitesurfers. Their inflatable bladders were light, strong and perfectly watertight. The technology was there, right in front of our eyes.
technology.title: Our Technology
technology.subtitle: 30 years of innovation for open-air cinema
technology.leger_desc: Our patented technology makes our screens 3 times lighter than the competition.
technology.montage_desc: Assembled in 10 minutes by 2 people, no tools required.
technology.garantie_desc: Our screens come with a 10-year parts and labour warranty.
technology.taille_desc: The largest inflatable screen in the world, with a 24-metre base.

usecases.section_desc: Our screens can be adapted to any context: from a private garden to a 50,000-seat stadium. Here are the most common uses.
usecases.cta_text: Do you have a different project? We can adapt.
usecases.cinema_desc: Festivals, summer screenings, film clubs. Our blower screens from 10 to 24m offer a perfect image even with a 5000 lumen projector. The airtight range (2 to 8m) is ideal for intimate screenings.
usecases.cinema_examples: Festivals, film clubs, summer screenings, open-air cinemas
usecases.festival_desc: Broadcasting artists on stage, broadcasting in real time to remote spectators. Our 15 to 24m screens provide perfect visibility from over 200 metres away.
usecases.festival_examples: Music festivals, concerts, shows and open-air operas
usecases.corporate_desc: Seminars, conferences, product launches. Our screens can be set up in 10 minutes in any space. The airtight range is silent (no fan), ideal for presentations.
usecases.corporate_examples: Seminars, conferences, team building, product launches, AGMs
usecases.mariage_desc: Create an unforgettable movie night. The airtight range is perfect: silent (no blower), lightweight, and set up in 5 minutes by just one person.
usecases.mariage_examples: Weddings, birthdays, garden parties, private parties, family celebrations
usecases.sport_desc: World Cup, Olympic Games, league matches - our giant screens can bring thousands of people together. The 24m format offers visibility of over 200 metres.
usecases.sport_examples: Fan zones, stadiums, bars and restaurants, public squares, municipal sporting events
usecases.drivein_desc: The drive-in format has undergone a spectacular revival. Our 10 to 15m blower screens are perfect for transforming a car park into a cinema. Set up in 30 minutes.
usecases.drivein_examples: Drive-in cinemas, summer tours, municipal events, shopping centres
usecases.hotel_desc: Luxury hotels and resorts are using our airtight screens to offer their guests cinema evenings. Their total silence and elegance make them a real asset to the customer experience.
usecases.hotel_examples: Ritz Carlton, seaside resorts, boutique hotels, holiday clubs, spas
usecases.ecole_desc: Student parties, student unions, end-of-year screenings, large-scale training sessions. Our screens can be set up quickly in a lecture theatre, a sports hall or a courtyard. The airtight (silent) range is ideal for conferences; the fan-cooled models are perfect for outdoor parties.
usecases.ecole_examples: Universities, prestigious higher education institutions, student unions, secondary schools, multi-purpose halls, sports halls
usecases.camping_desc: Boost your campsite’s appeal with a weekly film night. The airtight, silent system can be set up in just five minutes by a single person and can be installed right in the heart of the campsite without disturbing other guests’ sleep.
usecases.camping_examples: 4–5-star campsites, glamping, holiday villages, outdoor hotels, residential parks
usecases.title: They trust us
usecases.subtitle: Thousands of events organised around the world

story.title_before: From a beach in Hong Kong
story.intro_quote: "I have to admit I'm a bit lazy". But this apparent laziness conceals 30 years of relentless perseverance.
story.ch1992_title: Our first screen — 8 metres of aluminium
story.ch1992_text: An ultra-lightweight screen, featuring a structure of interlocking aluminium tubes and a stretched canvas with an 8-metre base. Weighing just a few kilos, it can be assembled in a matter of minutes by a single person. Surprisingly flexible, with exceptional wind resistance. Suitable for both indoor and outdoor use. This is the original prototype — proof that the idea works.
story.ch1992_alt: First Hallucine cinema screen — 8-metre aluminium tubular structure, founding prototype (1992)
story.ch1993_text: To learn how to erect structures, head to the village. The fairground workers, who are often looked down upon, are willing to share their knowledge. Two days of intensive training. At the end: “Now you’re a proper fairground worker; you can go on tour.”
story.ch1993_alt: Hallucine cinema screen mounted on an outdoor fairground structure — learning rapid assembly (1993)
story.ch1994_text: We’re thinking big: a 15-metre structure made from a lorry tarpaulin. On delivery, it weighed 600 kg. Impossible to handle without heavy machinery, impossible to assemble quickly on site. It was the most costly lesson in history: lightness isn’t an option, it’s fundamental. Everything that followed stemmed from this failure.
story.ch1994_alt: A 15-metre cinema screen made from a lorry tarpaulin, weighing 600 kg — the fatal mistake that sparked the quest for lightness in *Hallucine* (1994)
story.ch1995_text: In La Trinité-sur-Mer, in Brittany, a sail-making workshop became the birthplace of the first screens. Jean-Christophe, a master sail-maker, was an expert in sewing techniques. Together, they developed a revolutionary screen that could be folded away into a sail bag after each screening.
story.ch1995_alt: Inflatable cinema screen ‘Hallucine’ in a garden — Voilerie Bretonne, La Trinité-sur-Mer (1995)
story.ch2004_text: On a beach in Hong Kong, a kitesurf attracts attention. Its inflatable tubes, light and strong, gave rise to a crazy idea: what if we could show films in the open air with a screen as light as a sail?
story.ch2004_alt: Kitesurfing on a beach in Hong Kong — the inspiration for the inflatable tubes in the film *Hallucine* (2004)
story.ch2005_text: In Lyon, the former capital of fabric, an investigation leads to the best-kept secret of the automobile: the fabric used in airbags. A high-tenacity polyamide from DuPont de Nemours. Light, indestructible, perfect.
story.ch2005_alt: Exploded view of a airtight inflatable cinema screen, ‘Hallucine’ — made from high-tenacity polyamide fabric derived from car airbags (2005)
story.ch2010_text: Inspired by kitesurfing, the first airtight screens saw the light of day. Sealed air chambers, no blower, ultra-light. From 2m to 8m, they revolutionised the market.
story.ch2010_alt: Hallucine range of airtight inflatable cinema screens (5 and 6 metres) — sealed air chamber, no blower required (2010)
story.ch2020_text: February 2020, Shenzhen. COVID strikes. Impossible to return. Rather than do nothing, enrol at university to learn Chinese. China becomes a second home.
story.ch2020_alt: Stranded in Shenzhen during the COVID-19 pandemic — Hallucine continues operations from China, sourcing and manufacturing inflatable screens (2020)
story.chToday_text: From China, with a partner factory in Dongguan, Hallucine continues its mission to bring its screens to the widest possible audience. From Brittany to Shenzhen, the adventure continues.
story.chToday_alt: Three Hallucine inflatable cinema screens — 30 years of French innovation, manufactured by our partner factory in Dongguan
story.title: Our history
story.subtitle: 30 years of passion for open-air cinema

faq.section_desc: Answers to the questions we are most often asked.
faq.q1: What is an inflatable cinema screen?
faq.a1: An inflatable cinema screen is a lightweight, portable structure that inflates with air to create an outdoor projection surface. Unlike rigid screens or metal structures, it can be carried in a bag, set up in a few minutes and requires no tools or lifting equipment. Hallucine manufactures inflatable screens from 2 to 24 metres wide.
faq.q2: Why choose Hallucine rather than another manufacturer?
faq.a2: There are three main reasons. Firstly, our screens are 3 times lighter than those of our competitors, thanks to our technical fabrics. Secondly, we have 30 years' experience in the field. Thirdly, we offer a 10-year guarantee on the structure, which is unique in the industry.
faq.q3: What is the difference between your two ranges of screens?
faq.a3: The airtight range (2 to 8m) uses a sealed air chamber: you inflate once and the screen stays in shape all evening without electricity. The blower range (5 to 24m) uses a permanent blower for large formats. Both ranges come with a 10-year guarantee.
faq.q4: How much does a Hallucine inflatable screen cost?
faq.a4: The price depends on the size and technology chosen. Contact us for a free personalised quote. We also offer rental options for one-off events.
faq.a5: Yes, we deliver worldwide from our partner factory in Dongguan (China). Delivery times are 2 to 4 weeks depending on the destination. For Europe, allow around 2 weeks.
faq.q6: Do you also offer tents and inflatable furniture?
faq.a6: Yes, we use the same airtight technology to manufacture inflatable tents and event furniture (sofas, armchairs, bars, counters). Everything inflates in just a few minutes, without the need for a blower.
